[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Everglades Tips and Frequently Asked Questions
[METADATA/INTRO]: Here are some Everglades tips to help you plan your adventure as well as some of your most frequently asked questions: - When is the best time to visit the Everglades?...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Where to See the Alligators? 
 This is Gator country and you will find them all over the wetlands, especially close to bodies of water as they need it to regulate their body temperature. 
 The question is rather when to see them, the best chances to see alligators as well as other wildlife is during the dry season from end of November to April, when water levels are low, wildlife becomes more visible as they concentrate near the water holes. As water levels rise in the rainy season, the wildlife disperses and it becomes more difficult to spot them. 
 Look around in ponds, lakes, edges of rivers, and even man-made canals.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-04-21
[TITLE]: The Everglades: Much more than airboats and crocodiles 🐊
[METADATA/INTRO]: If you hear the word Everglades, your mind will most likely immediately conjure up the image of airboats about to take flight with their large propellers as they speed through the canals at breakneck speeds. You will also imagine daring...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: And this curious picture painted in green and blue, depicting mangroves, swamps, marshes, lakes, forests, sawgrass prairies, and tiny keys, is home to a multitude of animals, some of which are endangered. Alligators and American crocodiles are undoubtedly the most prominent stars; in fact, they say the Everglades is the only place in the world where both species coexist. 
 But watch out, for the elusive Florida panther also lurks here, the West Indian manatee swims, the raccoon prowls, the white-tailed deer survives, the otter explores, snakes hide, and all kinds of birds fly, from ospreys to ibises, herons, and even pelicans.
